title: High Protein Turkey Cauliflower Rice Skillet
metaDescription: One-pan ground turkey and cauliflower rice skillet with 38g protein and under 10g net carbs per serving. Ready in 25 minutes, no rice cooker needed.
targetKeywords: low carb high protein crockpot recipes
author: Recipe Team
keywords: high protein low carb, turkey cauliflower rice, ground turkey skillet

steps.name: Brown the turkey
steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground turkey, breaking it apart, and cook until no longer pink, about 6-7 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika.

steps.name: Cook the vegetables
steps.text: Push turkey to one side of the skillet. Add remaining oil, onion, garlic, and bell pepper to the other side. Cook 4-5 minutes until softened.

steps.name: Add the cauliflower rice
steps.text: Stir everything together and add the riced cauliflower. Cook for 5-6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cauliflower is tender but not mushy — it should still have a slight bite.

steps.name: Finish and serve
steps.text: Remove from heat, sprinkle cheddar on top, and let it melt for 1-2 minutes covered. Top with fresh cilantro before serving.

tips.question: Can I use fresh cauliflower instead of frozen riced cauliflower?
tips.answer: Yes — pulse a head of cauliflower florets in a food processor until rice-sized. Fresh cauliflower releases more water, so cook it 2-3 minutes longer to evaporate excess moisture.

tips.question: How do I keep the cauliflower rice from getting mushy?
tips.answer: Don't cover the skillet while it cooks and avoid overcrowding — cook in a wide skillet so the moisture evaporates instead of steaming the cauliflower.

tips.question: Can I make this dairy-free?
tips.answer: Yes, just skip the cheddar or use a dairy-free shredded cheese alternative — the recipe holds together fine without it.

tips.question: What's the best way to store leftovers?
tips.answer: Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days. Reheat in a skillet rather than the microwave to keep the cauliflower from turning watery.

storyIntro: Cauliflower rice gets a bad reputation for turning into a watery, sad pile — usually because it's steamed instead of actually cooked in a hot pan. This skillet fixes that by browning the turkey first, building a base of real flavor before the cauliflower ever hits the pan.
One pan, 25 minutes, 38g of protein and under 10g net carbs per serving — it's the kind of dinner that doesn't feel like a diet recipe even though it technically is one.
storyNote: The key is cooking the cauliflower rice uncovered in a wide skillet — trapping steam is what turns it mushy, not the cauliflower itself.
